item.title: Buckle Up or Pay the Price: RCMP Warns of Deadly Consequences
item.dc:creator: Greg Smith
item.description: Well now b'ys, you'd think after all these years folks would know to buckle up, but here we are, still talkin' about it. According to the RCMP—good ol' Corporal Jolene Garland—60 per cent of them poor souls who died in crashes this year weren't wearin' their seatbelts. You don't have to be a brain surgeon to figure out that clicking that belt could save your life, right? Sure, it don't make ya invincible; it's not like you're gonna walk away from every crash without a scratch. But, as Jolene puts it, they believe a lot of those deaths could've been avoided. So, seriously, strap in, would ya?
item.content:encoded: Well now b'ys, you'd think after all these years folks would know to buckle up, but here we are, still talkin' about it. According to the RCMP—good ol' Corporal Jolene Garland—60 per cent of them poor souls who died in crashes this year weren't wearin' their seatbelts. You don't have to be a brain surgeon to figure out that clicking that belt could save your life, right? Sure, it don't make ya invincible; it's not like you're gonna walk away from every crash without a scratch. But, as Jolene puts it, they believe a lot of those deaths could've been avoided. So, seriously, strap in, would ya?

item.title: Newfoundlander Reflects on Vienna Terror Scare After Taylor Swift Concerts Cancelled
item.dc:creator: Greg Smith
item.description:
Well b'y, have ye heard the latest goings-on? Three young fellas over in Austria got nabbed by the authorities 'fore they could pull off some sorta terrorist attack at three Taylor Swift concerts. Yessir, they were planning something nasty with knives or homemade bombs, all inspired by those Isis and al-Qaida crowd.
They went and cancelled all three shows in Vienna, leaving all the Swifties heartbroken, including some Newfoundlander bloke named Grant Dwyer and his family. They made the big trek all the way to Europe to see Taylor, and everything was going grand 'til the news broke just the night before. Talk about a trip gone sideways, eh?
Next day, more details came out and things started lookin' pretty grim. Poor ol' Dwyer and his two teenage daughters were disappointed, sure, but also relieved they didn't end up smack in the middle of a potential tragedy.
Now, they’re thinking about makin' their way to London for Taylor’s shows next week, 'cept this time they’ll have a pile more security around. Hopefully they won’t have to dodge any more shenanigans!
Anyway, there's the scoop. Might be worth listening to the full interview if you wants more details. Just make sure you're sittin' down with a cuppa tea, b'y!
item.content:encoded:
Well b'y, have ye heard the latest goings-on? Three young fellas over in Austria got nabbed by the authorities 'fore they could pull off some sorta terrorist attack at three Taylor Swift concerts. Yessir, they were planning something nasty with knives or homemade bombs, all inspired by those Isis and al-Qaida crowd.
They went and cancelled all three shows in Vienna, leaving all the Swifties heartbroken, including some Newfoundlander bloke named Grant Dwyer and his family. They made the big trek all the way to Europe to see Taylor, and everything was going grand 'til the news broke just the night before. Talk about a trip gone sideways, eh?
Next day, more details came out and things started lookin' pretty grim. Poor ol' Dwyer and his two teenage daughters were disappointed, sure, but also relieved they didn't end up smack in the middle of a potential tragedy.
Now, they’re thinking about makin' their way to London for Taylor’s shows next week, 'cept this time they’ll have a pile more security around. Hopefully they won’t have to dodge any more shenanigans!
Anyway, there's the scoop. Might be worth listening to the full interview if you wants more details. Just make sure you're sittin' down with a cuppa tea, b'y!

item.title: Woman Sought by RNC After Assault During Mount Pearl Home Break-In
item.dc:creator: Greg Smith
item.description:
Well, b'ys, hear dis one – the RNC's lookin' for a woman after she went and broke into someone's gaff in Mount Pearl. Imagine, some poor bugger gettin' walloped in their own home at eight in the mornin' on a Wednesday! Anyway, the troublemaker's name is Calystia Careen from Placentia, 35 years old. She's on the lam now, and they wants her for breakin' and enterin', assault, and mischief.
The coppers say it was likely targeted, so she wasn't just pickin' houses at random. If ye sees her, don't be a hero now, just give the police a bell. They're also askin' for any video footage to help nab her. Honestly, you'd think people would have better things to do at eight in the morning!
Stay safe out there, me son.
item.content:encoded:
Well, b'ys, hear dis one – the RNC's lookin' for a woman after she went and broke into someone's gaff in Mount Pearl. Imagine, some poor bugger gettin' walloped in their own home at eight in the mornin' on a Wednesday! Anyway, the troublemaker's name is Calystia Careen from Placentia, 35 years old. She's on the lam now, and they wants her for breakin' and enterin', assault, and mischief.
The coppers say it was likely targeted, so she wasn't just pickin' houses at random. If ye sees her, don't be a hero now, just give the police a bell. They're also askin' for any video footage to help nab her. Honestly, you'd think people would have better things to do at eight in the morning!
Stay safe out there, me son.

item.title: RCMP Dog Phox Apprehends Assault Suspect in Happy Valley-Goose Bay
item.dc:creator: Greg Smith
item.description:
Oh me nerves, b'y, you won't believe what went down in Happy Valley-Goose Bay the other night. So, there was this fella, name of Roderick Tshakapesh, who decided it'd be a great idea to leg it from the cops after they got called about some scrap he was involved in. This was around 9 p.m. on Saturday, so not exactly the perfect time for a jog, right?
Anyway, before he could get too far, Phox, one of them RCMP dogs with a nose the likes of which you've never seen, tracked him down in no time. The goof was trying to hide in the woods – like, come on, b'ys, you're not outsmarting a police dog.
So, they hauled him in, no drama or nothing, and slapped a few charges on him: assault causing bodily harm, uttering threats, and not playing by the rules of his release order. Now he's cooling his heels in custody until his court date on August 26.
Guess the fella learned the hard way that you can't outrun the long arm – or the four legs – of the law!
item.content:encoded:
Oh me nerves, b'y, you won't believe what went down in Happy Valley-Goose Bay the other night. So, there was this fella, name of Roderick Tshakapesh, who decided it'd be a great idea to leg it from the cops after they got called about some scrap he was involved in. This was around 9 p.m. on Saturday, so not exactly the perfect time for a jog, right?
Anyway, before he could get too far, Phox, one of them RCMP dogs with a nose the likes of which you've never seen, tracked him down in no time. The goof was trying to hide in the woods – like, come on, b'ys, you're not outsmarting a police dog.
So, they hauled him in, no drama or nothing, and slapped a few charges on him: assault causing bodily harm, uttering threats, and not playing by the rules of his release order. Now he's cooling his heels in custody until his court date on August 26.
Guess the fella learned the hard way that you can't outrun the long arm – or the four legs – of the law!

item.title: Churchill Park Businesses Gearing Up for Busy Concert Weekends
item.dc:creator: Greg Smith
item.description: Well now, b'ys, looks like the businesses 'round Churchill Park are gearin' up for a mad couple of weekends. Tens of thousands of folks are gonna be packin' the park for some concerts, with Nickelback, Pitbull, and Shania Twain headlin'. Big Ben's Pub in Churchill Square is expectin' a right crowd, people lookin' to wet their whistles before and after the shows. The owner, Ashley Ellis, says they're chompin' at the bit for the rush, especially from all the townies comin' in from over the overpass. She reckons the vibe 'round Churchill Square is somethin' special - high energy and all that, with music fillin' the air. Guess we'll see if the hype meets the hoopla, but sure, a bit of excitement don't hurt!
item.content:encoded: Well now, b'ys, looks like the businesses 'round Churchill Park are gearin' up for a mad couple of weekends. Tens of thousands of folks are gonna be packin' the park for some concerts, with Nickelback, Pitbull, and Shania Twain headlin'. Big Ben's Pub in Churchill Square is expectin' a right crowd, people lookin' to wet their whistles before and after the shows. The owner, Ashley Ellis, says they're chompin' at the bit for the rush, especially from all the townies comin' in from over the overpass. She reckons the vibe 'round Churchill Square is somethin' special - high energy and all that, with music fillin' the air. Guess we'll see if the hype meets the hoopla, but sure, a bit of excitement don't hurt!

item.title: Driver Pulled from Fiery Crash by Passerby Charged with Impaired Driving
item.dc:creator: Greg Smith
item.description:
Oh my son, you won't believe what happened over in Torbay last night! Some fella from Port Blandford was drivin' drunk and managed to roll his SUV right next to the fire hall. Yes b'ys, that's some grand planning there, eh? At least he knew where to crash. Anyway, the thing went up in flames, and another good Samaritan dragged him out before he became toast.
When the cops showed up, our drunken hero decided it'd be a good idea to refuse a breathalyzer, start throwin' punches at the officers, and makin' threats. Real charmer, that one! They brought him to the hospital with just minor injuries—lucky bugger.
Now he's got a whole grocery list of charges: drunk driving, assaultin' a police officer, utterin' threats, and refusin' the breathalyzer. Spent the night in lock-up and goin' to court today. Tune in to the Tim Powers Show on VOCM later, the guy who saved him's supposed to be on there chatting about the whole shebang. Gonna be some story, I tells ya!
item.content:encoded:
Oh my son, you won't believe what happened over in Torbay last night! Some fella from Port Blandford was drivin' drunk and managed to roll his SUV right next to the fire hall. Yes b'ys, that's some grand planning there, eh? At least he knew where to crash. Anyway, the thing went up in flames, and another good Samaritan dragged him out before he became toast.
When the cops showed up, our drunken hero decided it'd be a good idea to refuse a breathalyzer, start throwin' punches at the officers, and makin' threats. Real charmer, that one! They brought him to the hospital with just minor injuries—lucky bugger.
Now he's got a whole grocery list of charges: drunk driving, assaultin' a police officer, utterin' threats, and refusin' the breathalyzer. Spent the night in lock-up and goin' to court today. Tune in to the Tim Powers Show on VOCM later, the guy who saved him's supposed to be on there chatting about the whole shebang. Gonna be some story, I tells ya!

item.title: Judge Approves Postmedia Offer For Saltwire, Halifax Herald: Reports
item.dc:creator: Brian Callahan
item.description:
Alright, b'ys, here’s the scoop: Postmedia's gonna be takin' over Atlantic Canada's biggest newspaper chain, thanks to some judge in Nova Scotia givin' the nod. So, this big ol' company from Toronto is about to buy out SaltWire Network, the folks that run our very own St. John’s Telegram and the Halifax Herald.
The union, which stands up for the workers, agreed to some conditions from Postmedia to make the deal happen. But here’s the kicker, they’re ditching the companies’ pension plan! That’s gonna mess with over 400 people’s retirements. Lovely, eh?
As for how this is gonna affect the Telegram and its workers here in Newfoundland? No one’s said yet. Typical, just keep us in the dark, why don't ya? Guess we'll have to wait and see if we’re in for more trouble.
item.content:encoded:
Alright, b'ys, here’s the scoop: Postmedia's gonna be takin' over Atlantic Canada's biggest newspaper chain, thanks to some judge in Nova Scotia givin' the nod. So, this big ol' company from Toronto is about to buy out SaltWire Network, the folks that run our very own St. John’s Telegram and the Halifax Herald.
The union, which stands up for the workers, agreed to some conditions from Postmedia to make the deal happen. But here’s the kicker, they’re ditching the companies’ pension plan! That’s gonna mess with over 400 people’s retirements. Lovely, eh?
As for how this is gonna affect the Telegram and its workers here in Newfoundland? No one’s said yet. Typical, just keep us in the dark, why don't ya? Guess we'll have to wait and see if we’re in for more trouble.

item.title: Demand For Sold-Out Shows Attracting Scammers By The Droves
item.dc:creator: Brian Callahan
item.description:
Alright b'ys, listen up. There's this big hullabaloo in Churchill Park startin' tonight with a couple of weekends of concerts comin' up. Tonight's Nickelback—so if you're still into them, good on ya. Then you've got Pitbull tomorrow, and Shania Twain next weekend. Big names, right? So obviously, the scammers are crawling outta the woodwork like flies on a fish'n boat.
Organizers are warnin' everyone to be extra careful if you're thinkin' of buyin' resale tickets 'cause there's a load of fakes out there. They're sayin' don't be an eejit and send your hard-earned money to random folks on Facebook. Best bet is to stick with Showpass verified resale or get a ticket transfer from someone you can actually trust.
And remember, b'ys, if you gets scammed, the festival ain't takin' responsibility for your bad choices or your lost money. So, use yer noggins, and hopefully we'll see ya at the shows without a tale of woe to tell!
item.content:encoded:
Alright b'ys, listen up. There's this big hullabaloo in Churchill Park startin' tonight with a couple of weekends of concerts comin' up. Tonight's Nickelback—so if you're still into them, good on ya. Then you've got Pitbull tomorrow, and Shania Twain next weekend. Big names, right? So obviously, the scammers are crawling outta the woodwork like flies on a fish'n boat.
Organizers are warnin' everyone to be extra careful if you're thinkin' of buyin' resale tickets 'cause there's a load of fakes out there. They're sayin' don't be an eejit and send your hard-earned money to random folks on Facebook. Best bet is to stick with Showpass verified resale or get a ticket transfer from someone you can actually trust.
And remember, b'ys, if you gets scammed, the festival ain't takin' responsibility for your bad choices or your lost money. So, use yer noggins, and hopefully we'll see ya at the shows without a tale of woe to tell!

item.title: Dive, Ride, Run: St. John’s Triathlon Marks 40 Years of Endurance
item.dc:creator: Greg Smith
item.description:
Oh b'y, it's that time of year again! The St. John's triathlon's comin' back for its 40th year this weekend. Now, ya got yourself two ways to torture your body: the regular version with a 1.5 km swim, 46 km bike ride, and a 10 km run, or if you're outta your mind, you can do the super-duper tough one with a 2 km swim, 92 km bike, and then a half marathon just for kicks!
The real keeners doin' the long one, well, they might get a crack at representin' Canada at the World Championships in Spain come 2025. So, ya know, there's that bit of glory.
Anyhow, me buddy John Walsh, one of the organizers, is givin' a heads up that Sunshine Rotary Park’s gonna be a bit of a gong show with traffic. Bennett’s Road is gettin' the boot until 4 p.m., only local yahoos allowed. So, if you're plannin' on bein' in the area Sunday, pack a bit of patience and keep yer eyes peeled for them athletes, won't ya?
And I tell ya, it’s the only time of year you’ll see so many people willingly runnin’ and swimmin’ like someone’s chasin’ 'em—makes ya wonder what's goin' on in their noggins!
item.content:encoded:
Oh b'y, it's that time of year again! The St. John's triathlon's comin' back for its 40th year this weekend. Now, ya got yourself two ways to torture your body: the regular version with a 1.5 km swim, 46 km bike ride, and a 10 km run, or if you're outta your mind, you can do the super-duper tough one with a 2 km swim, 92 km bike, and then a half marathon just for kicks!
The real keeners doin' the long one, well, they might get a crack at representin' Canada at the World Championships in Spain come 2025. So, ya know, there's that bit of glory.
Anyhow, me buddy John Walsh, one of the organizers, is givin' a heads up that Sunshine Rotary Park’s gonna be a bit of a gong show with traffic. Bennett’s Road is gettin' the boot until 4 p.m., only local yahoos allowed. So, if you're plannin' on bein' in the area Sunday, pack a bit of patience and keep yer eyes peeled for them athletes, won't ya?
And I tell ya, it’s the only time of year you’ll see so many people willingly runnin’ and swimmin’ like someone’s chasin’ 'em—makes ya wonder what's goin' on in their noggins!

item.title: Provincial Government Opens Bids for Bailey Bridge in Marystown
item.dc:creator: Greg Smith
item.description: Oh b'y, would ya look at that. The provincial government's actually doin' somethin' useful fer once! They're acceptin' bids to throw up a bailey bridge over in Marystown. This temporary bridge is finally gonna give the folks some relief after they've been goin’ 'round the mulberry bush ever since the Canning Bridge got shut down back in February 'cause it was fallin’ apart. They'll be startin’ the work this fall now that they've got all their paperwork sorted. Meanwhile, they're still scratchin' their heads tryin' to figure out a permanent fix. Classic, eh? Always a temporary solution but never a permanent one in sight.
item.content:encoded: Oh b'y, would ya look at that. The provincial government's actually doin' somethin' useful fer once! They're acceptin' bids to throw up a bailey bridge over in Marystown. This temporary bridge is finally gonna give the folks some relief after they've been goin’ 'round the mulberry bush ever since the Canning Bridge got shut down back in February 'cause it was fallin’ apart. They'll be startin’ the work this fall now that they've got all their paperwork sorted. Meanwhile, they're still scratchin' their heads tryin' to figure out a permanent fix. Classic, eh? Always a temporary solution but never a permanent one in sight.
